sections.paragraph:
πότνια (πότνια, ης, ἡ,)
Etym. from same Root as πόσις, δεσπότης

a poet. title of honour, used chiefly in addressing goddesses or ladies:

1. = δέσποινα, mistress, queen, c. gen., πότνια θηρῶν (nom.) queen of wild beasts, Lat. potens ferarum, Il.; πότνια βελεων Pind.: absol., πότνι' Ἐρινύς Aesch.; often in voc., ὦ πότνι' Ἥρα Aesch.; ὦ πότνια (sc. Ἀθηναία) Ar.:—in pl. of the Eumenides, Hdt., Soph.; also of Demeter and Proserpine, Soph., etc.

2. as adj. revered, august, Hom.
